summary: Addresses both sourcery-ai[bot] review items: (1) tightened loop bounds (Phase 1: 30→15 iterations, Phase 2: 60→45 iterations) and increased script timeout from 120s to 150s for 30s safety margin with budget documented in comments, (2) replaced hardcoded replica count of 3 with dynamic reading from .spec.replicas plus a guard clause. The chainsaw-test.yaml changes are correct and well-implemented. However, docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md step table was not updated to reflect the new timeout (150s) and dynamic replica count, making it stale.

tests_checklist.item: Script Phase 1 confirms chaos effect before Phase 2 checks recovery
Guard clause validates DESIRED is non-empty and >= 1
All 6 Keystone condition assertions preserved verbatim (lines 107-119)
Timeout budget documented with worst-case analysis in comment (line 53-54)
Error messages are descriptive and include expected vs actual values

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows operator-pod-crash Deployment readyReplicas polling pattern
Comments reference CC-0076 and review #1 for traceability
No hardcoded replica counts — reads from .spec.replicas
${READY:-0} defensive defaults consistent with sibling tests

security_checklist.item: No security concerns — test-only YAML/shell with no secrets handling

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is resilient to pod replacement (Deployment-level polling)
Pattern consistent with operator-pod-crash and api-pod-kill-pdb
No files outside expected scope modified

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Single DESIRED variable eliminates all hardcoded replica references

fail_fast_checklist.item: DESIRED guard fails fast with clear error before polling begins
Phase 1 fails fast if chaos did not take effect after 30s
Phase 2 fails fast if recovery not observed after 90s

defensive_checklist.item: ${READY:-0} defensive defaults handle empty kubectl output
DESIRED validated for both empty and < 1 cases
Non-numeric DESIRED caught by -lt comparison failure propagating through || to guard clause

issues_found.description: docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md step table (line 198) is stale after review #1 changes. Three values need updating: (1) script timeout says '120s' but is now 150s, (2) 'drop below 3' should reference desired replicas read from .spec.replicas, (3) 'return to 3' should reference desired replicas.
issues_found.fix: Update the step 4 row: change `script (120s)` to `script (150s)` and change 'drop below 3' / 'return to 3' to 'drop below desired' / 'return to desired (read from .spec.replicas)'.
issues_found.evidence: | 4 | Verify chaos effect and assert no-regression | `script` (120s) + `assert` (5m) | Polls Memcached Deployment `readyReplicas` to confirm chaos took effect (drop below 3) and recovery completed (return to 3)...

suggested_improvements: The CC-0076 timeout/loop-bound feedback was appended to .planwerk/review_patterns/parallel-test-execution-with-shared-mutable-state.md, but it concerns script timeout budgeting — not parallel execution or shared mutable state. Consider creating a separate review pattern (e.g., 'test-script-timeout-must-exceed-worst-case-loop-duration') so future reviews can detect this category independently.
next_steps: Update docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md line 198: change timeout from 120s to 150s and replace hardcoded '3' references with dynamic replica phrasing to match the implementation
title: Fix race condition in memcached-pod-kill chaos test
